content: Drafting two papers: AI restoration of war photographs (ethics + vignette study) and RRT with a vector maze formulation. Public repos in motion: TTorch (C++/Python autograd), Desir (agentic assistant), a real-time emotion CV stack, and a maze-solving visualizer. Tightening this minimal site to match reality.

Third place at Innovate Bay (BayHawk 2.0) for a wildfire stack: YOLOv8 + NASA FIRMS + weather context, fused with a multi-agent LLM pipeline (PydanticAI, GPT-4o, FastAPI) for severity and response planning.

Won ThinkNext 2026 with Uniflow — a PydanticAI-orchestrated career copilot for SFBU: goal + résumé agents, RAG over courses/events, and a voice/text mock interview path with STAR-style feedback (Streamlit demo).
HackHayward weekend: shipped KryptosProof — multi-agent, tool-using red team (sqlmap / Nuclei / FFUF), Docker sandbox, live dashboard, and signed reports for the vibe-coding security angle.
Competed at ICPC Pacific Northwest Regional 2025 (Division 2). Serving as Robotics Club president this year while keeping coursework and build projects moving.

Joined Quiche (ADU marketplace) as a founding member — AI / DevOps focus: MCP server for contextual retrieval, multi-agent assistant design, and GCP (Compute Engine, Cloud Run, Cloud SQL) for production-shaped deploys.
Second place at BayHack / Innovate Bay (BayHawk 1.0) with Bit for Bit: phone receipt capture → structured MongoDB + assistant layer for small-team inventory (OpenAI API, FastAPI, Node, Python).
Started as a student assistant in the SFBU IT department — Tier-1 triage for hardware/software/network tickets plus asset tracking across campus.
Finished the Sofeast LLM internship (May 2022–Aug 2025): internal RAG/LLM systems, evaluation and deployment tooling, and shipping models into real business workflows.
Top 3 at SFBU IEEE Mind Sparks with BleuLaMuse — GPT + FAISS + Streamlit career chatbot with modular backend for later cloud expansion.
